channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Adobe patches seven max severity ColdFusion, Campaign flaws
item.description: Adobe has released security patches for seven maximum-severity vulnerabilities in the ColdFusion web app development platform and the Campaign Classic marketing automation platform. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Anthropic Restores Claude Fable 5 After U.S. Lifts Jailbreak-Linked Export Controls
item.description: Anthropic is putting Claude Fable 5 back online worldwide. On June 30, the U.S. Commerce Department lifted the export controls it had imposed on Fable and its more tightly controlled sibling Mythos 5 ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "massive, ongoing, automated password spray attack" aimed at Microsoft's Azure command-line interface (CLI), compromising dozens of accounts in the process. ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Researcher Analyzes 3,000 Live ClickFix Payloads, Exposing API-Driven Malware Delivery
item.description:
ClickFix, the trick that fools people into running malware by hand, has quietly grown a back office.
New research shows the malicious commands behind its fake "prove you're human" pages are now hande...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Citrix Patches Six NetScaler Flaws Allowing File Read and Denial-of-Service
item.description: Citrix on Tuesday released security updates to address multiple flaws in NetScaler ADC (formerly Citrix ADC) and NetScaler Gateway (formerly Citrix Gateway) that could be exploited by an attacker to f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: China-Linked Group Targets Southeast Asia Critical Systems
item.description: The group compromised at least 10 regional organizations, including two state-owned entities, and deployed a new backdoor....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Anthropic to restore Claude Fable access on Wednesday
item.description: Anthropic has confirmed that the Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude's two most powerful models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Anthropic rolls out Sonnet 5 with near-Opus 4.8 performance at a lower price
item.description: Anthropic is now rolling out Sonnet 5, and it's almost as good as the Opus range, but it is designed to be cheaper than the company's flagship model. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New BioShocking attack manipulates AI browser into data theft
item.description: A new prompt injection attack dubbed "BioShocking" could trick AI-powered browsers into treating real-world risky actions as part of a fictional scenario, causing them to ignore any safety guardrails....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake Bug Report Hijacks AI Coding Agents at Scale
item.description: "Agentjacking" is the latest demonstration of how easily attackers can exploit an AI agent's inability to differentiate between content and instructions....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft accelerates quantum-safe roadmap as risks grow
item.description: Microsoft announced today that it is accelerating its quantum-safe security roadmap, saying advances in quantum computing are bringing the need to replace today's encryption standards sooner than prev...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Malicious PyPI packages give hackers control of Telegram bot servers
item.description: A campaign active since last November has been targeting Python developers building Telegram bots with trojanized Pyrogram forks that allow attackers to read arbitrary files on compromised servers. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Attackers Hijack Exposed AI Endpoints to Power Offensive Ops
item.description: Attackers don't need any special authentication to reach a target endpoint — they just need to know where it is....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Why Identity Security Is Your Cyber Career Entry Point
item.description: As AI reshapes cybersecurity workflows, John Paul Cunningham, CISO at SIlverfort, says the technology is creating opportunities rather than eliminating jobs — and there are more ways than ever to brea...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Phishers Gain Persistence at EU, Asia Hospitality Orgs
item.description: Separate but similar campaigns described by Microsoft and Trend Micro use malicious zip files to spread malware via social engineering and obsfucation, including blockchain abuse....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
item.description: New Microsoft research shows how attackers can hijack AI agents that act on a user's behalf, using nothing more than a poisoned tool description to make the agent quietly hand over company data to an ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: RustDuck Botnet Rebuilds in Rust to Hijack Routers and Servers for DDoS
item.description: A new two-stage malware family called RustDuck is hijacking home routers, IP cameras, Android boxes, and poorly secured servers, then stitching them into a network built to knock websites and online s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Langflow RCE Exploited to Deploy Monero Miner on Exposed AI App Endpoints
item.description:
Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical Langflow vulnerability as part of fresh attacks designed to deliver a Monero cryptocurrency miner.
The activity has been found to weaponize CVE-2026...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fake Perplexity extension on Chrome Web Store tracked searches
item.description: A malicious extension in the Chrome Web Store is masquerading as the Perplexity AI answer engine, intercepting search traffic and collecting browsing information. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Silent Swap Crypto Clipper Uses Fake Google Notes Extension to Replace Wallet Addresses
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an active browser extension campaign that is designed to steal cryptocurrency by stealthily replacing wallet addresses when unsuspecting users initiate a transac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GuardFall Exposes Open-Source AI Coding Agents to Decades-Old Shell Injection Risks
item.description:
The safety check that is supposed to stop an AI coding agent from running a dangerous command can be walked straight past using a shell trick that has been public for decades.
New research from Adver...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Lessons from the Underground: How to Combat Business Email Compromise
item.description: Business Email Compromise is more than an email scam. It's a coordinated operation involving compromised accounts, financial research, and cash-out networks. Flare explores how underground forums reve...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
item.description:
Researchers tested 444 AI chatbot apps for iPhone and found that 282 of them, nearly two-thirds, exposed paid AI access through their network traffic.
In many cases, the path in was visible just by w...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AI-Generated Workflows Are a Silent Security Disaster
item.description: Teams are dealing with a truly dangerous problem — automation that works, but that no one understands....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: What the Numbers Say About FIFA 2026 Cyber Risk
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 opened on June 11. By that date, according to Check Point Research, the fraud infrastructure targeting it had already been built, staged, and partially deployed. Threat actor a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Attackers Exploit SimpleHelp CVE-2026-48558 to Deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer
item.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed exploiting a recently disclosed maximum-severity security flaw in SimpleHelp to deliver two previously unreported malware families, TaskWeaver and Djinn Steal...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Insurance giant Aflac discloses data breach after subsidiary hack
item.description: American insurance giant Aflac has disclosed a new data breach after attackers breached its Japan subsidiary's systems and stole personal and bank account information. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft adds smarter bot protection to Teams meetings
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new Teams admin policy that allows organizers to prevent third-party bots from joining meetings without approval. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Kali Linux 2026.2 released with 9 new tools, NetHunter updates
item.description: Kali Linux 2026.2, the second release of the year, is now available for download, featuring 9 new tools and numerous Kali NetHunter improvements. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Blackfield ransomware asks Nidec Corporation for $2 million ransom
item.description: The Blackfield ransomware gang is asking for a $2 million ransom from Nidec Corporation, a large Japanese manufacturer of electronic components for automotive and computing applications. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
